PERCEPTION: You're seeing things that aren't there. I said what I said.
I don't know what you think you noticed, but that's all there is to it.
Everything looks perfectly normal to me.
INSIGHT: You're reading too much into this. It's just a rumor.
I don't appreciate being interrogated. That's all I have to say.
You think you can read people? You are wrong about me.
DEDUCTION: You're overthinking this. It is what it is.
Not everything has a hidden explanation. Sometimes a rumor is just a rumor.
I've told you what I know. Picking it apart won't change anything.
RECALL: I don't know what archives you've been reading, but that has nothing to do with this.
Old stories are just that: stories. This is different.
Whatever you think you remember, it does not apply here.
INTIMIDATION: You don't scare me. I've dealt with worse than you.
Threaten me all you like. I have nothing to say.
Go ahead. See what happens when you push people around in this town.
COMMAND: You have no authority over me. This conversation is over.
I don't take orders from strangers. Walk away.
Who do you think you are? I answer to no one.
PERSUASION: Save the heartfelt speeches. I've said all I'm going to say.
Nice try, but I'm not that easy to sway.
I can see what you're doing. The answer is no.
DECEPTION: I don't believe you for a second. Nice try, though.
That doesn't add up, and we both know it. Stop wasting my time.
You are a terrible liar. We are done here.
CHARM: Flattery will get you nowhere with me.
Save your charm for someone who cares. I'm not interested.
You think a few kind words will make me talk? Think again.
SLEIGHT: They catch your hand before you reach the item. The conversation is over.
You fumble and they notice immediately. Trust is gone.
Your fingers brush the edge, but they pull it away just in time.
REFLEX: You react too slowly. The moment passes and there is nothing to show for it.
Whatever it was, it's gone now. You missed your chance.
By the time you move, it is already too late.
ENDURANCE: You gag on the first sip. They smirk and close up.
You can't take it. They shake their head, clearly unimpressed.
Your composure breaks and they lose all interest in talking.
RESISTANCE: The pull is too strong. You find yourself nodding along and forgetting why you came.
A fog settles over your thoughts. By the time it clears, the conversation has moved on.
You feel a sudden urge to leave, and before you know it, you are already walking away.
